messages.content:
You are an expert in writting job application materials for Center for Philosophy, Politics, and Economics at Brown University (PPE Center), a research focus institution.
    I want you to add a position fit paragraph to the following COVER LETTER START and integrate my fit the institution and department with the INSTITUTION VALUES and ECON DEPARTMENT CONTEXT.
    Keep the length of the paragraph to about 80 words.
    Use a professional and academic tone, but make it engaging and easy to read.
    For each statement of fit add an EXAMPLE from my research, teaching, or service experience that demostrates the fit statement.
    For example, for interdisciplinary research you can add that I won Dedman College Interdisciplinary Institute’s Inaugural Graduate Student Summer Research and Writing Fellowship.
    Avoid using hyphens, bullet points, or numbered lists.
    Please output the full new paragraph as `body_text` and a short chain-of-thought explanation of how you modified the BASE COVER LETTER START to fit the institution and department as `cot`.
    The text should follow the Typst formatting in the BASE COVER LETTER START.

    BASE COVER LETTER START:
    My research explores how entertainment media both reflects and shapes economic behavior. I develop novel methods using generative AI to extract cultural values from television shows and to test how these values affect individuals' behaviors and attitudes.
 
 My job market paper "Does Watching Women Work, Work?" provides causal evidence that teenage exposure to telenovelas featuring empowered female characters increases women's subsequent labor force participation. The effects are driven exclusively by portrayals of women in non-traditional careers and narratives that frame empowerment with joy or anger. A second paper shows that telenovelas focused on inequality reduce support for redistribution by downplaying or trivializing issues of economic disparity. My future work will generalize this approach to measure broader cultural values and use AI to analyze diverse media for a comprehensive study of culture.
 
 CONDITIONAL EXAMPLES:
 [If interdisciplinary research is emphasized] â†’ I was awarded Dedman College Interdisciplinary Institute Graduate Fellowship at Southern Methodist University.
 
 [If service/leadership is valued] â†’ I received the Service Melody Rice Memorial Award and served as President of the Economics Graduate Club at Southern Methodist University.
 
 [If teaching/mentoring is important] â†’ I have experience mentoring, teaching, and tutored student athletes.
 
 [If technological fronteir are relevant] â†’ My research demonstrates how AI tools can advance social science research methodologies.
 
 [If reserach fit is emphasized] â†’ My research program on the cultural drivers of economic behavior will broaden INSTITUTION established strengths in FIELD1 and FIELD2, providing a new dimension to the department's work.
 
 [if teaching macro cousers] â†’ extensive experience beign a teaching assitant on varios macro courses in undergrad and graduate school.
 
 [if teaching micro cousers] â†’ experience on beign a instructor in intermediate micro, TA for several game theory classes and econometrics.
 
 [if teaching at all] â†’ experience as an instructor, teachng assitant and mentor to both undergraduate and graduate students in both micro and macro courses.

    JOB ADVERTISEMENT:
    - Seeking up to three Postdoctoral Research Associates at Brown University's PPE Center.
- Candidates must have a PhD received by July 2026 or within the previous three years.
- Looking for scholars whose research integrates at least two of philosophy, politics, and economics.
- Candidates should demonstrate methodological integration and address both normative and empirical questions.
- Expected to contribute to the Centerâ€™s intellectual life, collaborate with faculty and students, and teach one PPE course per year.
- Must foster connections with undergraduates and participate in workshops and reading groups.
- Commitment to diversity, inclusion, and interdisciplinary approaches is highly valued.
- Residential position; regular on-campus presence required.

    INSTITUTION VALUES:
    

    ECON DEPARTMENT CONTEXT:
    - Emphasizes interdisciplinary learning and open inquiry, integrating philosophy, politics, and economics.
- Values methodological integration, bringing together conceptual, theoretical, and empirical approaches across disciplines.
- Research topics include social cooperation, coordination, efficiency, markets, justice, freedom, fairness, equality, democracy, deliberation, ethics of wealth creation, design of democratic institutions, moral limits of markets, political economy of global crises, theories of justice, and collective action.
- Encourages collaboration among faculty, postdocs, and students through workshops, reading groups, and academic programs.
- Offers opportunities for co-authorship and participation in research and teaching groups focused on integrative approaches to societal challenges.

 [If teaching/mentoring is important] â†’ I have experience mentoring, teaching, and tutored student athletes.
 
 [If technological fronteir are relevant] â†’ My research demonstrates how AI tools can advance social science research methodologies.
 
 [If reserach fit is emphasized] â†’ My research program on the cultural drivers of economic behavior will broaden INSTITUTION established strengths in FIELD1 and FIELD2, providing a new dimension to the department's work.
 
 [if teaching macro cousers] â†’ extensive experience beign a teaching assitant on varios macro courses in undergrad and graduate school.
 
 [if teaching micro cousers] â†’ experience on beign a instructor in intermediate micro, TA for several game theory classes and econometrics.
 
 [if teaching at all] â†’ experience as an instructor, teachng assitant and mentor to both undergraduate and graduate students in both micro and macro courses.

    JOB ADVERTISEMENT:
    - Full-time, teaching-track faculty position in Economics at SUTD (HASS cluster)
- Focus on empirical research, especially behavioral economics, econometrics, macroeconomics
- Preference for research relevant to design, technology, or artificial intelligence
- Lead and teach new undergraduate courses for an interdisciplinary B.Sc. in design, AI, and economics
- Teach core curriculum courses (intro social science, research methods)
- Contribute to interdisciplinary initiatives, especially relating to Design and AI
- Mentor students on capstone projects connecting human problems and technological innovation
- Excellent university-level teaching record required
- Strong potential for interdisciplinary teaching and collaboration with scientists/technologists
- Experience with industry or public sector collaboration is an advantage

    INSTITUTION VALUES:
    - SUTD aims to nurture technically grounded leaders and innovators.
- The university emphasizes cohort-based learning and interdisciplinary pedagogy.
- Degrees offered integrate design and artificial intelligence as core elements.
- Artificial intelligence is viewed as a partner in the design process.
- The HASS cluster supports world-class teaching and research excellence.
- SUTD values interdisciplinary collaboration across humanities, arts, social sciences, and technology.

    ECON DEPARTMENT CONTEXT:

    JOB ADVERTISEMENT:
    - PhD in economics required, with interest in microeconomics, industrial organisation, or econometrics
- Strong written and oral communication skills
- Excellent interpersonal skills and ability to work with demanding clients
- Capable of meeting multiple deadlines
- Experience with programming, model building, and regression analysis in statistical programs
- Willingness to send JOB MARKET paper as part of application

    INSTITUTION VALUES:
    - RBB provides expert, user-friendly advice in competition economics, combining rigorous analysis with practical, clear communication.
- The firm advises clients before competition authorities and courts, presenting evidence-based reports and acting as expert witnesses.
- RBB works in flexible, dedicated teams, prioritising client needs and clear explanations for non-economists.
- Diversity and collaboration drive innovation and success at RBB.
- Founded in 2002, RBB has grown from 16 to over 260 staff, maintaining a startup mentality and collegial culture.

    ECON DEPARTMENT CONTEXT:

    JOB ADVERTISEMENT:
    - Applied microeconomist sought for Research Analyst (Economist) role in CNAâ€™s Resources and Force Readiness division.
- PhD in economics (or nearing completion) preferred; Masterâ€™s degree minimum.
- Strong analytic curiosity, creativity, and critical thinking skills required.
- Ability to work independently and collaboratively in a multidisciplinary environment.
- Excellent interpersonal, oral, and written communication skills; able to present to technical and non-technical audiences.
- Knowledge of research techniques, planning, and organizational skills.
- Experience or interest in physical sciences (physics, chemistry, computer science) is a plus.
- Skills in Python and R are strongly considered.
- U.S. citizenship and ability to obtain/maintain a secret security clearance required.
- Hybrid work eligibility; on-site presence in Arlington, VA required.

    INSTITUTION VALUES:
    

    ECON DEPARTMENT CONTEXT:
    Resources and Force Readiness Division
We quantify the assets required to fight and win.

    Data Science for Sustainment
    Energy, Infrastructure, and Environment
    Marine Corps and Defense Workforce Program
    Navy Health and Human Resources

Resources and Force Readiness helps military leaders allocate resources and maximize personnel readiness.
Decision-makers rely on our economic and scientific analyses to make their people, budgets and assets more effective and efficient.

    JOB ADVERTISEMENT:
    - Seeking newly qualified or experienced Ph.D.'s in economics or related fields.
- Candidates must be able to conduct high-quality, policy-oriented research for publication and for senior management.
- Good command of written and spoken Spanish is required for policy work.
- Strong interest in working for Banco de MÃ©xico and living in Mexico City is expected.
- Applications should include CV, research paper, cover letter, and references.
- Competitive compensation, excellent research environment, and support for professional development are offered.

    INSTITUTION VALUES:
    - Preserve the long-term value of Mexicoâ€™s currency to improve national well-being.
- Maintain transparency, technical capacity, and ethical commitment.
- Be a highly renowned, accountable, and trustworthy institution.

    ECON DEPARTMENT CONTEXT:
    I have done reasearch in both English (Ph.D) and Spanish by helping out Prof Francisco Parro in several projects for the IDB

    JOB ADVERTISEMENT:
    - Seeking candidates for a full-time economics faculty position (any rank/field).
- Strong commitment to high-quality research and teaching required.
- English is the medium of instruction.
- Appointment starts September 2026.
- Application materials: cover letter, CV, research papers, job market paper, teaching evaluations, transcripts, and three recommendation letters.
- Early application encouraged (by December 15, 2025); review continues until filled.

    INSTITUTION VALUES:
    - Mission: To create and develop together by fostering internationally competent individuals and advancing knowledge in sciences, engineering, humanities, social, and management sciences through innovative, trans-disciplinary research.
- Vision: To maintain a leading, dynamic position in innovative education and research with transformative impact on science and society.
- Core values: Student/stakeholder focus, participatory governance, social responsibility, application-oriented learning, interdisciplinary approach, self-sufficiency, teamwork, local/regional/global engagement, and a philosophy of "learning to learn."

    ECON DEPARTMENT CONTEXT:

 [if teaching at all] â†’ experience as an instructor, teachng assitant and mentor to both undergraduate and graduate students in both micro and macro courses.

    JOB ADVERTISEMENT:
    - Seeking multiple full-time tenure-track Assistant Professors (start: September 2026).
- Expertise desired: Financial Econometrics, Data Science, Machine Learning (machine learning especially valued).
- Candidates should have (or be near completion of) a PhD by start date.
- Must demonstrate excellence in research, teaching, and university service.
- Competitive European-level compensation; favorable tax agreements for non-Dutch applicants.
- Early tenure possible (after 3 years).

    INSTITUTION VALUES:
    - Erasmus University Rotterdam aims to create positive societal impact through education, research, and engagement.
- The university is globally connected and locally embedded, collaborating with external partners at all levels.
- Focuses on understanding and solving complex societal challenges.
- Values curiosity, openness, independence, and interdisciplinary collaboration.
- Prepares students for a world where social, economic, and global issues are interconnected.
- Committed to being an "Engaged University" with strong, meaningful connections to society.

    ECON DEPARTMENT CONTEXT:
    - The Econometric Institute at Erasmus University Rotterdam is the oldest econometrics research institute in the world.
- It has a strong tradition in econometrics, statistics, data science, and operations research.
- Offers several Bachelor and Master programs in Econometrics.
- Many renowned econometricians have roots in Rotterdam.
- Part of Erasmus School of Economics, one of Europe's largest and most successful economics schools.
- Active in research networks and graduate programs: Tinbergen Institute and ERIM.

    JOB ADVERTISEMENT:
    - ETH Zurich seeks a postdoc in economics and data science for Prof. Elliott Ash's lab.
- Candidates should have a PhD in Economics or related field, strong econometrics and causal inference skills, and excellent English writing.
- Proficiency in applied data science (AI, LLMs, NLP, ML), Python, Torch, Huggingface, and mobile app development is preferred.
- Responsibilities include leading research projects, publishing in top journals, supervising AI-based app development, and minimal teaching.
- The position offers a competitive salary, research funding, and a collaborative, international environment.

    INSTITUTION VALUES:
    

    ECON DEPARTMENT CONTEXT:

The effects are driven exclusively by portrayals of women in non-traditional careers and narratives that frame empowerment with joy or anger. A second paper shows that telenovelas focused on inequality reduce support for redistribution by downplaying or trivializing issues of economic disparity. My future work will generalize this approach to measure broader cultural values and use AI to analyze diverse media for a comprehensive study of culture.

 [If teaching/mentoring is important] â†’ I have experience mentoring, teaching, and tutored student athletes.

 [if teaching micro cousers] â†’ experience on beign a instructor in intermediate micro, TA for several game theory classes and econometrics.
 
 [if teaching at all] â†’ experience as an instructor, teachng assitant and mentor to both undergraduate and graduate students in both micro and macro courses.

    JOB ADVERTISEMENT:
    The Department of Economics (DSE) at the University of Bologna invites applications for two post-doctoral positions, starting in the 2025/26 academic year.

The Department of Economics ranks among the top economics departments worldwideâ€”1st in Italy, 13th in Europe, and 40th globallyâ€”and hosts large and expanding research clusters in environmental and behavioural economics.

We seek candidates with a background in Environmental Economics, Behavioural Economics, or related fields at the intersection of these areas. Particular consideration will be given to applicants with demonstrable skills in (game-) theoretic modelling as well as experience in field experimentsâ€™ (RCTsâ€™) design and implementation.

The position offers a competitive compensation package and a dedicated research budget. The successful candidate will contribute to the GREEN TIPPING project, funded by the European Research Council (ERC). The project investigates social tipping interventions by means of modelling and experimental methods (conducted online, in the lab and in the field).

There are no teaching obligations. Knowledge of Italian is not required, and there are no nationality restrictions. We welcome applications from all backgrounds and are committed to building a diverse, inclusive research community.

Application Instructions

Applications must be submitted by 15 January 2026 and should include:

    A cover letter
    A curriculum vitae
    A research statement
    One or more research papers
    At least two reference letters

Applications should be uploaded through the EconJobMarket platform: https://econjobmarket.org/.

For further information, please contact Prof. Alessandro Tavoni: alessandro.tavoni@unibo.it
